q: Why are ecosystems considered open systems?
options: They have no boundaries and merge freely with other ecosystems
Both energy and matter can enter and exit; sunlight enters, heat is lost, and chemical elements cycle between the ecosystem and atmosphere or hydrosphere
They are open only to energy; matter is always conserved within a single ecosystem
Decomposers keep the system open by continuously breaking down matter and releasing it as heat
explanation: Open systems exchange both energy and matter with their surroundings; ecosystems receive solar energy, lose heat, gain CO2/nutrients from outside, and lose gases and leached minerals.
